ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to salted egg yolk cookies; 'Biscuits and cookies' (A0CKN) used as closest parent category.

serving.description: 1 cookie (approximately 1 piece from a box of 12)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one individually wrapped cookie weighing about 25 g, representing one of twelve pieces in a standard Sinpopo box.

nutrient_source.title: Cookies, butter, commercially prepared (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 18158) adapted for salted egg yolk cookie profile; sodium adjusted upward to reflect salted egg yolk content
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy butter cookie profile used as the closest defensible analogue for a baked shortbread-style cookie. Salted egg yolk cookies share a similar butter-flour-sugar-egg base. Sodium has been adjusted upward (from ~300 mg to ~480 mg per 100 g) to reflect the savoury salted egg yolk component, consistent with published analyses of salted egg yolk snack products. Saturated fat is elevated relative to plain butter cookies due to egg yolk fat contribution. All other macros are within the expected range for this cookie class.

gi_source.title: Shortbread biscuit / butter cookie GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for salted egg yolk cookies. The GI of 55 is estimated from shortbread and butter biscuit analogues reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables (GI range 40–64 for butter/shortbread biscuits; median ~55). Salted egg yolk cookies share a similar high-fat, moderate-sugar, refined-flour base which suppresses GI relative to plain crackers. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 15.25) / 100 ≈ 8.4, where carbohydrate per 25 g serving = 61.0 × 0.25 = 15.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from salted egg yolk
hypertension.guidance: Each 25 g cookie provides approximately 120 mg sodium; consuming multiple pieces from a 12-cookie box can contribute 360–720 mg sodium, representing 16–31% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board, and individuals with hypertension should strictly limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate and sugar content with moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 55 (low category) and a glycemic load of ~8.4 per cookie, occasional single-piece consumption is less acutely glycaemic than plain crackers, but the high energy density (498 kcal/100 g) and sugar content (22 g/100 g) require careful portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat and cholesterol from butter and salted egg yolk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salted egg yolk cookies are high in saturated fat (~14.8 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~95 mg/100 g) due to the butter and egg yolk base; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions in line with the Singapore HPB guideline of keeping saturated fat below 10% of total daily energy intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salted egg yolk cookies are a popular Singaporean baked snack product made from a rich shortbread-style dough incorporating cured salted duck egg yolks and aromatic fresh curry leaves. The cookies are baked until golden and have a crumbly, melt-in-the-mouth texture characteristic of high-butter shortbread, with a distinctive savoury-sweet flavour profile derived from the intensely umami salted egg yolk balanced against a lightly sweet buttery base. Curry leaves contribute a subtle herbal fragrance. Sinpopo Brand, a well-known Singaporean café and heritage food brand, popularised this format as a premium gift-box item sold in individually wrapped pieces. Nutritionally, these cookies are energy-dense (approximately 498 kcal per 100 g), high in saturated fat from butter and egg yolk, and contain elevated sodium from the salted egg component. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low category) due to the fat-rich matrix slowing glucose absorption, but the high caloric density and saturated fat content make them a treat food requiring moderation. Clinically relevant for hypertension due to sodium load, and for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and cholesterol. Suitable for vegetarians; contains gluten, egg, and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a salted egg yolk cookie?
Is salted egg yolk cookie healthy?
Sinpopo salted egg yolk cookie nutrition
Can diabetics eat salted egg cookies?
Salted egg yolk cookie saturated fat

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for Sinpopo's specific salted egg yolk cookie product is available in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy butter cookie profile with sodium adjustment to reflect the salted egg yolk component. GI is estimated from shortbread/butter biscuit analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the macro profile is well-grounded in a defensible analogue, but product-specific formulation differences (exact egg yolk ratio, sugar level, curry leaf quantity) cannot be verified without a laboratory analysis or manufacturer disclosure.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy butter cookie data (FDC ID 172779) with sodium elevated to ~480 mg/100 g to reflect the savoury salted egg yolk component, consistent with published ranges for salted egg yolk snack products. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are carried from the USDA SR Legacy butter cookie profile. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not meaningfully present in baked butter cookies and is not reported in the USDA source for this food category. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry used. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry used. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry used. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry used. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry used. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry used. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from shortbread and butter biscuit data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. Halal status assumed based on typical Sinpopo product positioning; not independently verified. Food group reclassified from input 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Snacks' as this is a baked cookie product, not a primary dairy or egg food.
